posts.text: Over three years into the job that he covered for so long, the walls are closing in on Malaysia’s veteran prime minister. Register for free to learn why he only has himself to blame https://www.economist.com/asia/2026/06/25/two-state-elections-may-break-malaysias-ruling-coalition?taid=6a41d6571e0c30000155ffa5&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text: Fear is building beneath the market’s surface: The Fear & Greed Index is now down to 24.8, the lowest since early April. This metric is now below the 30-point threshold, signaling "Extreme Fear" among investors. The reading comes despite the S&P 500 sitting just -3.7% below its all-time high. By comparison, the index bottomed at 5.8 points on March 30th and 5.2 points in November 2024. This comes as the 5-day average put-to-call ratio is up to 0.84, the highest since the 2nd week of April and above the levels seen between July 2025 and January 2026. Keep watching sentiment.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text: Very, very interesting. If Trump/US is behind this coup, it would be one hell if a pincer move that even Hannibal would be proud of: Bait the enemy with a fake retreat down the center: - Come out with a controversial MOU that even folks in his own party denigrate as a TACO… - … yet mysteriously exempt Israel from withdrawing from Lebanon… - …while baiting Iran into aggressive chasing moves thinking they got the better hand Flank an over-committed and overconfident Iran with a pincer move from two sides: - Meanwhile, Israel/Lebanon make peace and ally against Hezbollah, forming one arm of the pincer… -…while fomenting a coup in Iraq against pro-IRGC elements, forming the second arm of the pincer
posts.author: hkuppy
